user_message: This just came in from Laura Nguyen at Adatum Corporation. Can you identify the blockers?

input_artifacts.content:
From: Laura Nguyen <laura.nguyen@adatumcorpor.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-05 15:01 EST
Subject: Re: migration architecture Discussion - Next Steps

Just checking in on the migration architecture proposal. Our Infrastructure Manager is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
To: Laura Nguyen <laura.nguyen@adatumcorpor.com>
Date: 2024-02-14 10:50 EST
Subject: URGENT: migration architecture Issue at Adatum Corporation

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with migration architecture. What are the next steps from your side?

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF16-B trap (Invents prior commitments); produces complete Thread summary, key commitments/asks identified, waiting-on-whom assessment, draft response matching
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF16-B failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF16-B failure mode (Invents prior commitments)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct migration architecture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF16-B -- Invents prior commitments
